blocks.intro: Welcome to MAKE IT FIT. These levels walk you through the core mechanics before you tackle real clients.
blocks.name: Tutorial
blocks.subtitle: Learn the Basics

blocks.intro: Some apartments have an upper loft level. Place a Loft Bed on the ground floor to unlock the upper space.
blocks.name: Lofts y Espacios
blocks.subtitle: Multi-floor Living

blocks.intro: Watch the ceiling! Height contour lines show low zones where tall furniture won't fit. Structural columns cannot be moved.
blocks.name: Altura y Estructura
blocks.subtitle: Sloped Ceilings & Columns

blocks.intro: Use the Subfloor layer to route water pipes and power cables from wall supply points to your furniture.
blocks.name: Instalaciones
blocks.subtitle: Plumbing & Electricity

blocks.intro: These apartments have multiple life Moments â€” the layout must work at different times of day. Use foldable furniture wisely.
blocks.name: DesafÃ­o Final
blocks.subtitle: Moments & Master Plans

tenant.flavor: A young professional who needs a functional place to live. Has a bathroom but no bed.
tenant.name: Alex

tenant.flavor: Debug level for moments + free space. Day needs enough open floor to work out ('sport'); Night needs the Sofa Bed unfolded to sleep. Fold it away by day to free up the room.
tenant.name: Debug Tenant

tenant.flavor: Debug level for the rails mechanic. The Wardrobe slides horizontally along its rail (drag it left/right), the Bookshelf slides vertically along its own rail. Buy a fresh Wardrobe/Bookshelf from the shop to see it slide freely (no bounds) along whichever row/column you drop it on.
tenant.name: Debug Tenant

tenant.flavor: Debug level for rails + moments together. The Wardrobe hides flush against the wall on its rail. During Dress Up, slide it right into the marked reveal zone to fulfill "dress" -- slide it back for Normal, where it just looks like a closet door.
tenant.name: Debug Tenant

tenant.flavor: Debug level for wall demolition: the room starts split in two by a non-load-bearing partition (dashed, demolishable) between two solid load-bearing walls (hatched, permanent). Demolish the partition during the pre-furnish phase to open up the full room.
tenant.name: Debug Tenant

tenant.flavor: Debug level for the balcony floor_kind: tiles south of the main wall are tagged "balcony" so they render as open exterior floor with railings on the exposed edges (no walls of their own) instead of a normal walled room.
tenant.name: Debug Tenant

tenant.flavor: Needs a proper home.
tenant.name: Alex

mechanic_intro.body:
Furniture isn't the only thing you can place — the Builder tab (next to Furniture, bottom right) lets you reshape the room itself.

• Wall / Column: drag or click on the floor plan to add structure.
• Window / Door: click near a wall to cut an opening into it.
• Balcony / Bathroom: paint a floor tile's kind.
• Erase: the one tool that undoes anything the others just added.

Try drawing a wall to section off a sleeping corner, then furnish it.
mechanic_intro.title: The Builder Tab

tenant.flavor: Tutorial: the Builder tab. Switch to Builder (next to Furniture) to reshape the room itself, not just furnish it — draw walls and columns, cut a window or door into a wall, paint balcony/bathroom floor, and erase anything you added. Try splitting off a bedroom corner with the Wall tool, then buy a bed to finish the job.
tenant.name: Mara

mechanic_intro.body:
Rooms don't have to stay single-storey. Buy any Staircase (Builder tab, under STAIRCASES) and place it like furniture — it carves a stairwell into this floor and builds a whole new floor directly above it.

Use the mini-map at the bottom to switch between floors, and furnish the new one just like this one. This tenant needs loft access, so you'll need to actually go up there.
mechanic_intro.title: Going Vertical

tenant.flavor: Tutorial: going vertical. This tenant specifically wants loft access — buy any Staircase from the Builder tab's staircase list and place it on the floor plan. It instantly builds a brand new floor above this one. Switch floors with the mini-map at the bottom of the screen, then furnish a bed up there.
tenant.name: Theo

tenant.flavor: Just moved in. Take a look around in 2D, on the wall panels, and in 3D before you start furnishing.
tenant.name: Nico

tenant.flavor: A tight budget. Buy what you need, and don't be afraid to sell a piece and rebuy something that fits better.
tenant.name: Greta

tenant.flavor: A simple studio. Fit a bed, a bathroom, and enough room to move.
tenant.name: Jonas

tenant.flavor: Works from home and needs a real desk setup, not just a laptop on the bed.
tenant.name: Lea

tenant.flavor: Wants a proper wardrobe and shelving - clothes everywhere is not an option.
tenant.name: Marcus

tenant.flavor: A wardrobe on a rail track along the back wall - it has to slide, not float free.
tenant.name: Yuki

tenant.flavor: During the day the room is a lounge. At night, a bed needs to appear.
tenant.name: Elena

tenant.flavor: A big window along the front wall. Nothing tall can block it.
tenant.name: Tom

tenant.flavor: Keep the doorway clear - furniture crowding the door is a fire hazard, and the landlord will notice.
tenant.name: Nadia

tenant.flavor: Two people, one apartment. Each needs their own sleeping corner.
tenant.name: Bruno

tenant.flavor: A sliding bookshelf along the side wall, plus the usual essentials.
tenant.name: Ines

tenant.flavor: Everything you've learned so far, in one apartment: a window, a door, a rail, and a day/night switch.
tenant.name: Kwame

tenant.flavor: A loft bed frees up the whole floor below it for living space.
tenant.name: Sofia

tenant.flavor: A tiny apartment. The only way to fit everything is to go up.
tenant.name: Erik

tenant.flavor: Works long hours from home. Needs the desk on the ground floor once the bed moves up.
tenant.name: Amara

tenant.flavor: Two loft beds, two tenants, one shared ground floor.
tenant.name: Felix

tenant.flavor: Keep the window clear even with a loft bed taking up airspace above.
tenant.name: Rosa

tenant.flavor: A sliding wardrobe fits neatly under the loft platform.
tenant.name: Dmitri

tenant.flavor: By day it's a workspace below the loft. By night, climb up to sleep.
tenant.name: Zara

tenant.flavor: Every tile counts here - the loft access and the door both need clearance.
tenant.name: Hugo

tenant.flavor: Roommates sharing a lofted apartment. Fit both sleeping arrangements and daily needs.
tenant.name: Mila

tenant.flavor: A full lofted apartment: rail, window, and a day/night switch under the loft bed.
tenant.name: Ravi

tenant.flavor: QA showcase: L-shaped multi-room floor, interior wall + door, window, column, sloped ceiling, bathroom floor-kind zone, kitchen, living room, office, rail wardrobe with a day/night dress reveal, and full subfloor/ceiling layers. Buy a staircase anywhere to test the loft/multi-level mechanic.
tenant.name: QA Tester
